name: Mexican Pork Roast (Cooked in Beer with Green Sauce)
recipeIngredient: 1 loin or shoulder pork roast, (4 to 5 lb.)
recipeInstructions:
1. Place onions and carrots in a roasting pan.
2. Rub the pork with salt, oregano, and coriander.
3. Place the pork on top of the vegetables.
4. Add beer; cover.
5. Roast at 350 degrees for 2 hours and 15 minutes.
6. Add more beer if necessary.
7. Slice and serve the pork with hot dip or green sauce, rice, and refried beans.
Note: This may also be cooked in a slow cooker on a low setting with 2 cups of beer for 8 to 12 hours.
Green Sauce:
1. Heat oil in a small skillet.
2. Saute onion and garlic until tender.
3. Drain the tomatillos; reserve the liquid.
4. In an electric blender, combine the tomatillos, reserved liquid, onion, garlic, olive oil, oregano, and cilantro.
5. Puree the mixture.
6. Heat the skillet again over moderate heat.
7. Pour in the sauce; cook for 10 minutes.
8. Remove from heat; add wine vinegar, salt, and pepper.
9. Chill the sauce and serve with meat dishes.
